incorrects.description: Student selected 10, likely subtracted 5 instead of finding difference between 30 and 15

params.description: Tacos and Salad bars pulse to draw attention.

steps.text: Look at where each bar ends on the axis. Tacos reaches 30. Salad reaches 15. What's the difference between those two numbers?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30, reading Tacos value instead of finding the difference

steps.text: The question asks how many MORE Tacos got than Salad. That means we need the difference. Tacos is 30, Salad is 15. How far apart are they?

incorrects.description: Student selected 45, added the two values instead of finding the difference

steps.text: When we ask how many MORE, we subtract to find the difference. We don't add them together. What's 30 minus 15?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual bracket appears connecting Tacos bar at 30 and Salad bar at 15, showing the gap of 15.

steps.text: Let me show you. Tacos ends at 30 on the axis. Salad ends at 15. To find how many more, I subtract: 30 minus 15 equals 15. The difference between the two bars is 15.
